_meta.description: Sponsored Products ad-group-level negative keywords. Daily snapshot — every fire upserts the full current set. Sourced via Ads-API v3 paginated POST /sp/negativeKeywords/list (NOT a report endpoint). Use to gate search-term mining queries: LEFT JOIN ON (campaign_id, ad_group_id, lower(keyword_text)) WHERE state='ENABLED' AND r.keyword_id IS NULL surfaces terms not yet negated.
_meta.retention_note: rows older than 2 days are pruned nightly (newest snapshot per store never pruned where applicable); query beyond the window returns a report_date_note

columns.comment: Amazon Ads profile id; one row set per (profile, snapshot_date).

columns.note: Marketplace this ads row belongs to. Query ads data per (store, country_code) directly — no need to reverse-map profile_id.

columns.comment: Marketplace-local date the snapshot was captured. Filter `WHERE snapshot_date=(SELECT MAX(snapshot_date) FROM rpt_sp_negative_keywords WHERE store_id=...)` for current state; older snapshot_dates are historic.
Server-side timestamp the row was UPSERTed.
Amazon's keyword identifier — stable across snapshots.
Campaign id this negative scopes to.
Ad group id this negative scopes to. NULL if Amazon ships only campaign scope (rare for ad-group endpoint — typical case is a populated value).
The negated term verbatim. Compare against search-term mining via lower(keyword_text).
'NEGATIVE_EXACT' | 'NEGATIVE_PHRASE'. Determines how Amazon matches the negation.
'ENABLED' | 'PAUSED' | 'ARCHIVED' | 'USER_DELETED'. Only ENABLED actually blocks impressions; filter accordingly.
Locale string Amazon ships with the negative (e.g. 'en_US'). Often NULL for older negatives.
Any Amazon response fields not promoted to a typed column (createdDate, lastUpdatedDate, etc.).
columns.note: Documented opt-outs (2026-07-11): nativeLanguageKeyword / nativeLanguageLocale — localization metadata on a handful of rows, deliberately not promoted.
